pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 88 creates range(1, 51) = 50 data rows, plus 1 header row
Code analysis: setup_task.sh headers list contains 9 items: SKU, Product Name, Category, Description, Unit Price, Stock Qty, Supplier, Warehouse Location, Notes

pi_items.metadata_value: Some columns are way too wide (6cm from setup script)
pi_items.verified_value: 6cm = 2.36 inches (not excessively wide by typical standards, but causes overflow on A4 landscape)
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh defines ColWide style with 6cm width

pi_items.verified_value: 12.78 inches total (2×6cm wide + 2×3.5cm medium + 5×2.5cm narrow = 12.78 inches)
pi_items.source: Code analysis and manual calculation from column width definitions

pi_items.verified_value: Portrait (verifier.py line 69 defaults to 'portrait' - no explicit orientation set in generated ODS)
pi_items.source: Code analysis: OpenDocumentSpreadsheet() in setup_task.sh does not set orientation, so ODF default applies

pi_items.verified_value: 100% scale (default, no scale-to or scale-to-X attributes set)
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh does not set scaling properties, verifier.py defaults to 100%
Code analysis: ODF specification defaults to 2cm margins. Verified against verifier.py line 74-77 defaults and actual ODS file inspection

pi_items.metadata_value: A4 (from README.md context of professional spreadsheets)
pi_items.source: Code analysis: verifier.py line 79-80 defaults show 'page_width': '21cm', 'page_height': '29.7cm' which matches A4

pi_items.verified_value: Approximately 1.9 pages at 100% scale in portrait with 2cm margins, or 1.3 pages at landscape with 2cm margins
pi_items.source: Calculation: A4 portrait is 21cm (8.27") wide minus 4cm total margins (2cm left + 2cm right) = 6.69" usable. Content is 12.78", so 12.78 ÷ 6.69 = 1.91 pages. A4 landscape is 29.7cm (11.69") minus 4cm margins = 10.12" usable, so 12.78 ÷ 10.12 = 1.26 pages. The task description claim of 3-4 pages is not supported by the actual column widths and default margins.
Code analysis: verifier.py line 444 checks 'if total_score >= 75:'
Code analysis: verifier.py check_landscape_orientation() line 180 returns 20 for passing
Code analysis: verifier.py check_scaling_configuration() returns up to 25 points
Code analysis: verifier.py check_column_widths() line 235 returns 20
Code analysis: verifier.py check_margins() line 264 returns 15
Code analysis: verifier.py estimate_horizontal_pages() line 324 returns 20

pi_items.verified_value: 0.5-1.0 inches for full credit; 0.4-1.1 inches for reasonable credit
pi_items.source: Code analysis: verifier.py line 260 sets is_reasonable = 0.4 <= avg_margin <= 1.1, is_optimized = 0.5 <= avg_margin <= 0.9

pi_items.verified_value: 70-95% for full credit; fit-to-pages also acceptable
pi_items.source: Code analysis: verifier.py line 194 checks if 70 <= scale_percent <= 95

pi_items.metadata_value: No excessively wide columns >5 inches (from README.md)
pi_items.verified_value: >5 inches OR >2.5x average width considered excessive
pi_items.source: Code analysis: verifier.py line 231 checks 'max_width > 5.0 or max_width > (avg_width * 2.5)'

privileged_info_summary: The print_layout_crisis task uses synthetic data: an ODS spreadsheet with 51 rows (1 header + 50 data rows) and 9 inventory columns, created programmatically with intentionally poor layout (12.78 inches total width requiring scaling/optimization). Default ODS configuration is portrait orientation with 2cm (0.787 inch) margins and 100% scaling. The verifier checks 5 criteria (landscape: 20 pts, scaling 70-95%: 25 pts, column optimization: 20 pts, margins 0.5-1.0": 15 pts, horizontal pages ≤2: 20 pts) against a 75-point threshold. NOTE: Task JSON contains no explicit 'metadata' field; these verified values are derived from task description, README, setup script, and verifier code.
